subject: Looking to a better future
body: Plus: A satirical debut, and the story of the physicist who invented the atomic bomb ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Books By Deborah Dundas After a week where climate change was top of mind with thick wildfire smoke blanketing the GTA, it makes sense to look to the future — how human nature might shape it, and how we can work together to make it better. In “Our Tribal Future,” University of Toronto biological anthropologist David R. Samson examines how we are naturally inclined to form groups. In a Q&A with the Star, Samson argues that “maladaptive tribalism” leads to negative outcomes like xenophobia and the spread of conspiracy theories. But tribalism can also be a force for good, bringing co-operation and altruism. And he should know. He is “is one of only a handful of scientists who have done field research among both human forager societies and wild chimpanzees” and grew up in a fundamentalist cult in Indiana, our interviewer, Brian Bethune, points out. “From the beginning, the tribal drive has been bug and feature, curse and blessing,” Samson told the Star. “Bringing it to people’s conscious awareness, I believe, is the only shot our species has of reducing intergroup conflict in the future.” There is more book news and reviews below, too, to get you started on a week of reading. Join us again next Friday for more from the world of books. Creating a better future: How to take our instinct to belong to a group and turn it into a force for good In his new book ‘Our Tribal Future,’ David R. body: お知らせいたします。 お客様の MyJCB アカウントの認証情報が、2025年6月3日（火）に期限を迎えます。現在、ご利用は継続中です。 多くの会員様が新生活シーズンの安全なご利用のために登録を完了されています。24時間以内に以下のリンクより最新情報の登録をお願いいたします。情報は厳重に保護されています。 認証情報を更新 登録後、次回ご利用時に特典をご用意しております。ご登録がない場合、アカウントが制限される可能性があります。 今後ともよろしくお願い申し上げます。 本メールは自動送信専用です。ご返信はご遠慮ください。 © 2025 JCB株式会社. All Rights Reserved。

subject: Støtteteam.
body: Kjære bruker, Vi vil gjerne informere deg om at postkassen din er utdatert. Vi oppdaterer sikkerheten til alle postkasser. Klikk her eller https://shorturl.at/xcMfH for å laste inn din UIO-webmail på nytt og oppdatere den med den siste sikkerhetsoppdateringen. Vi sletter alle ubrukte e-postkontoer for å gi plass til nye kontoer. Hvis denne postboksen fortsatt er aktiv, vennligst oppdater kontoen din ved å klikke på koblingen ovenfor. Hvis postkassen din ikke er oppdatert innen de neste 48 timene, vil e-postkontoen din bli ansett som inaktiv og vil derfor bli slettet fra vår database. Takk. støtteteam, UIO ©️ Webmaster - Tjenester ©️ Internett

subject: Problem with Chef PostgreSQL Integration
body: Hello Customer Support, I am encountering difficulties with the integration between Chef and PostgreSQL. The process halted during the synchronization phase, which I believe could be because of mismatched API versions or incorrect setup. Kindly inform me if you require additional information from me.

subject: Bank of America Alert: update Your Account Information
body: Dear Bank of America valued member, Due to concerns regarding your recent activites involving your account and for the safety and integrity of the online banking community we have issued this warning message. It has come to our attention that your account information needs to be updated due to inactive member status, frauds and/or spoof reports. We will appreciate if you could spend several minutes out of your online experience and renew your records to prevent your account running into future problems or even failure with the online service. However, failure to update your records will result in temporary/permanent account suspension. This notification expires Sept 17, 2006. Once you have updated your account records your internet banking service will not be interrupted and will continue as normal. Just follow the link below and renew your account information.

subject: svn commit: r581644 - in /spamassassin/trunk: lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Plugin/BodyRuleBaseExtractor.pm sa-compile.raw
body: Author: jm Date: Wed Oct 3 09:17:10 2007 New Revision: 581644 URL: http://svn.apache.org/viewvc?rev=581644&view=rev Log: add a --quiet switch to sa-compile to make things less noisy Modified: spamassassin/trunk/lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Plugin/BodyRuleBaseExtractor.pm spamassassin/trunk/sa-compile.raw Modified: spamassassin/trunk/lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Plugin/BodyRuleBaseExtractor.pm URL: http://svn.apache.org/viewvc/spamassassin/trunk/lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Plugin/BodyRuleBaseExtractor.pm?rev=581644&r1=581643&r2=581644&view=diff ============================================================================== --- spamassassin/trunk/lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Plugin/BodyRuleBaseExtractor.pm (original) +++ spamassassin/trunk/lib/Mail/SpamAssassin/Plugin/BodyRuleBaseExtractor.pm Wed Oct 3 09:17:10 2007 @@ -52,6 +52,7 @@ # $main->{bases_can_use_quantifiers} = 0; # /foo.*bar/ or /foo*bar/ or /foooo?bar/ # $main->{bases_can_use_char_classes} = 0; # /fo[opqr]bar/ # $main->{bases_split_out_alternations} = 1; # /(foo|bar|baz)/ => ["foo", "bar", "baz"] +# $main->{base_quiet} = 0; # silences progress output # TODO: it would be nice to have a clean API to pass such settings # through to plugins instead of hanging them off $main @@ -65,7 +66,7 @@ my $self = $class->SUPER::new($mailsaobject); bless ($self, $class); - $self->{show_progress} = 1; # default + $self->{show_progress} = !$mailsaobject->{base_quiet}; # $self->test(); exit; return $self; @@ -85,7 +86,9 @@ my $main = $conf->{main}; if (!$main->{base_extract}) { return; } - info("base extraction starting. this can take a while..."); + $self->{show_progress} and + info("base extraction starting. this can take a while..."); + $self->extract_set($conf, $conf->{body_tests}, 'body'); } @@ -110,7 +113,7 @@ my $count = 0; my $start = time; $self->{main} = $conf->{main}; # for use in extract_hints() - info ("extracting from rules of type $ruletype"); + $self->{show_progress} and info ("extracting from rules of type $ruletype"); # attempt to find good "base strings" (simplified regexp subsets) for each # regexp. We try looking at the regexp from both ends, since there @@ -368,7 +371,7 @@ } my $elapsed = time - $start; - info ("$ruletype: ". + $self->{show_progress} and info ("$ruletype: ". (scalar keys %{$conf->{base_string}->{$ruletype}}). " base strings extracted in $elapsed seconds\\n"); } Modified: spamassassin/trunk/sa-compile.raw URL: http://svn.apache.org/viewvc/spamassassin/trunk/sa-compile.raw?rev=581644&r1=581643&r2=581644&view=diff ============================================================================== --- spamassassin/trunk/sa-compile.raw (original) +++ spamassassin/trunk/sa-compile.raw Wed Oct 3 09:17:10 2007 @@ -87,6 +87,7 @@ GetOptions( 'list' => \\$opt{'list'}, 'sudo' => \\$opt{'sudo'}, + 'quiet' => \\$opt{'quiet'}, 'keep-tmps' => \\$opt{'keep-tmps'}, 'configpath|config-file|config-dir|c|C=s' => \\$opt{'configpath'}, @@ -131,6 +132,8 @@ # at least info $opt{'debug'} ||= 'info'; +my $quiet = $opt{'quiet'} || 0; + # ensure the body-rule base extractor plugin is loaded, we use that my $post_config = q( loadplugin Mail::SpamAssassin::Plugin::BodyRuleBaseExtractor @@ -160,6 +163,7 @@ $spamtest->{bases_can_use_quantifiers} = 0; $spamtest->{bases_can_use_char_classes} = 0; $spamtest->{bases_split_out_alternations} = 1; +$spamtest->{base_quiet} = $quiet; if (defined $opt{'updatedir'}) { $opt{'updatedir'} = Mail::SpamAssassin::Util::untaint_file_path($opt{'updatedir'}); @@ -251,14 +255,21 @@ # compile it... - chdir $dirpath; print "cd $dirpath\\n"; + chdir $dirpath; + $quiet or print "cd $dirpath\\n"; rule2xs($basespath); + my $log = ""; + if ($quiet) { + $log = ">>$dirpath/log"; + open (ZERO, ">$dirpath/log"); close ZERO; # empty it + } + run(get_perl()." Makefile.PL ". - "PREFIX=$dirpath/ignored INSTALLSITEARCH=$installdir"); + "PREFIX=$dirpath/ignored INSTALLSITEARCH=$installdir $log"); - run("make"); - run($sudo."make install"); # into $installdir + run("make $log"); + run($sudo."make install $log"); # into $installdir # and generate the bases.pl file, for perl consumers @@ -273,7 +284,8 @@ } if (!$opt{'keep-tmps'}) { - chdir '/'; print "cd /\\n"; # saves trouble on MacOS, possibly + chdir '/'; + $quiet or print "cd /\\n"; # saves trouble on MacOS, possibly run($sudo."rm -rf $dirpath"); # cleanup } else { @@ -283,9 +295,15 @@ sub run { my @cmd = @_; - print join(' ',@cmd)."\\n"; + $quiet or print join(' ',@cmd)."\\n"; system(@cmd); - ($?>>8 != 0) and die "command failed!"; + if ($?>>8 != 0) { + if ($quiet) { + die "command '".join(' ',@cmd)."' failed!"; + } else { + die "command failed!"; + } + } } sub get_perl { @@ -332,7 +350,8 @@ $force and rmtree $PATH; mkdir $PATH or (!$force and die "mkdir($PATH): $!"); - chdir $PATH; print "cd $PATH\\n"; + chdir $PATH; + $quiet or print "cd $PATH\\n"; my $cprefix = $modname; $cprefix =~ s/[^A-ZA-z0-9]+/_/gs; @@ -725,6 +744,10 @@ Use C to run code as 'root' when writing files to the compiled-rules storage area (which is C<@@LOCAL_STATE_DIR@@/compiled/@@VERSION@@> by default). + +=item B<--quiet> + +Produce less diagnostic output. Errors will still be displayed. =item B<--keep-tmps>

subject: INTERN JOB POSITION
body:
CORNELL UNIVERSITY

Department of Mathematics

Student Services,

Cornell University, Student Services, urgently requires a Research Assistant for a Remote Position to earn $380 weekly.

The Student Services Recruitment Specialist,  [name] is currently working on Research and would require the services of Students (Graduate/Undergraduate) of any Department of the Institution to Obtain a Remote Position in this Research. Slots are limited and acceptance will be given on a first come first serve basis.

To proceed with the application process, contact  [name] via email at [name]@gmail.com using your alternate email stating your full name, email address, year of study, and department regarding the job description and further application requirements.

[name]

Associate Professor

Faculty,

Department of Mathematics,

CORNELL UNIVERSITY.

subject: Issue with Multiple App Integration
body: Dear Customer Service Team, I am contacting you to inform you about a problem I am experiencing with the integration of several applications, which has abruptly stopped working. It is believed that this issue might be due to recent API modifications or an overload on the server. Despite restarting services, reviewing logs, and testing each application independently, the issue remains unresolved. I would be grateful if you could examine this situation and offer a solution at your earliest convenience. Please inform me if you require any further information from me to address this problem. Thank you for your time and cooperation. Sincerely, [Your Name]
